name: PubChemPy (PubChem Database Access for Python)

auth.notes: No auth required; PubChem PUG REST API is public

performance.notes: Network-bound; PubChem rate limits apply (5 requests/second)

agent_readiness.notes: Access PubChem's 116M+ compound database. import pubchempy as pcp. compounds = pcp.get_compounds('aspirin', 'name'). compound = compounds[0]. compound.cid. compound.iupac_name. compound.canonical_smiles. compound.molecular_formula. compound.molecular_weight. compound.isomeric_smiles. compound.xlogp. compound.h_bond_donors. pcp.get_compounds('CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O', 'smiles'). pcp.download('SDF', 'aspirin_3d.sdf', 'aspirin', 'name', record_type='3d'). Substance search. Substructure/similarity search.

gotchas: PubChem rate limit is 5 requests/second — throttle bulk queries
Multiple compounds can match a name — check CID carefully
best_when: Looking up chemical properties by name, SMILES, or CID
Downloading compound data (SMILES, InChI, molecular formula, properties)
Bulk compound property retrieval for drug discovery datasets
Converting between compound identifiers (name ↔ SMILES ↔ CID)
avoid_when: High-throughput queries (>1000/min) — use PubChem FTP directly
When ChEMBL data is preferred (use chembl_webresource_client)
Real-time production queries without rate limit handling
summary: PubChemPy accesses PubChem's 116M+ compound database. pcp.get_compounds('aspirin', 'name'). compound.canonical_smiles, .molecular_weight, .xlogp. SMILES → CID lookup. 3D structure download. MIT by Matt Swain. Best for compound property lookup by name/SMILES. Rate limit: 5 req/s. Unmaintained since 2017 — no async. For bulk data, use PubChem FTP.
